sn22.20:1.1: Near Sāvatthī. There the Blessed One addressed the monks,
sn22.20:1.3: Whatever cause & condition there is for the arising of form, that, too, is not-self.
sn22.20:1.4: Being brought into play by what is not-self, how could form be self?
sn22.20:1.9: Whatever cause & condition there is for the arising of consciousness, that, too, is not-self.
sn22.20:1.10: Being brought into play by what is not-self, how could consciousness be self?
sn22.20:1.11: Seeing thus, the instructed disciple of the noble ones grows disenchanted with form, disenchanted with feeling, disenchanted with perception, disenchanted with fabrications, disenchanted with consciousness. Disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate. Through dispassion, he is released. With release, there is the knowledge, ‘Released.’
sn22.20:1.12: He discerns that ‘Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done. There is nothing further for this world.’”
